title: hibbertia axillaris
intro: Hibbertia axillaris, commonly known as Guinea flower, is a small, native Australian plant that belongs to the Dilleniaceae family. The plant has been used in traditional Aboriginal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are believed to aid in wound healing and reducing fever. The health benefits of Hibbertia axillaris include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity, making it a promising natural remedy for various health issues. Hibbertia axillaris is typically prepared as a tincture or infusion for medicinal use, although further research is needed to fully understand its effects and optimal preparation methods.
